ai_summary: Fedbank Financial Services reported a strong Q1 FY '25, with Asset Under Management (AUM) growing 40% year-on-year, significantly driven by its gold loan business, and Profit After Tax increasing 30% year-on-year alongside an improved cost-to-income ratio. However, the company experienced a deterioration in asset quality, with Gross Non-Performing Assets (GNPA) rising by 30 basis points and credit costs nearly doubling quarter-on-quarter, primarily due to higher mortgage delinquencies and one-off write-offs, which management attributes to Q1 seasonality but warrants monitoring. Additionally, new RBI guidelines on fair practices pose potential revenue and asset quality impacts for the NBFC sector during the transition period.
